front: What is an address in networking?
back: An address is a way to identify a device so data can be sent to it.
So data knows where to go and is sent to the correct device.
A network is a group of devices connected together so they can communicate.

front: Does every device have a built-in identity?
back: Yes, every device has a unique hardware address built into it.
A MAC address is a unique code built into a device to identify it on a local network.

front: Where is a MAC address stored?
back: It is stored in the device’s network interface card (NIC).

front: Do devices use the same address everywhere?
back: No, devices use different addresses depending on the network.
An IP address is an address used to send data to the correct device.
No, an IP address can change depending on the network.
A MAC address is a unique identifier built into a device’s network interface card (NIC), used to identify it on a local network.
An IP address is a unique address used to identify a device and send data to the correct destination.

front: What is the difference between a MAC address and an IP address?
back: A MAC address identifies a device on a local network, while an IP address is used to send data across networks and the internet.
